For k=0: multiples 28 and 30. Four consecutive numbers containing both: {28,29,30,31}, {27,28,29,30}. Both are valid. {27,28,29,30} contains 28 (div by 7) and 30 (div by 6). Valid. Similarly {28,...,31}.

For k=1: multiples 42*1 +28=70 and 42*1 +30=72. Four consecutive numbers containing both: {69,70,71,72}, {70,71,72,73}. Both valid.

Therefore, Case 4 gives S: {27,28,29,30}, {28,29,30,31}, {69,70,71,72}, {70,71,72,73}.

Case 5: 7b -6a=3.

Solve 7b -6a=3.

7b =6a +3 => 7b ≡3 mod6 => 7b ≡1b ≡3 mod6 => b≡3 mod6. Therefore, b=6k +3.

Substitute back into 7b=6a +3:

7*(6k +3)=6a +3 => 42k +21=6a +3 => 6a=42k +18 => a=7k + 3.

Therefore, general solution: a=7k +3, b=6k +3.

Multiples:

6a=6*(7k +3)=42k +18

7b=7*(6k +3)=42k +21

Therefore, multiples are 42k +18 and 42k +21. Difference is 3.

Check two-digit numbers:

Lower bound: 42k +18 ≥10 => k≥0.

Upper bound: 42k +21 +3 ≤99 => 42k +24 ≤99 => 42k ≤75 => k ≤1 (since 42*2=84, 84 +24=108>99). Therefore k=0 and 1.

For k=0: multiples 18 and 21. Four consecutive numbers containing both: {18,19,20,21}, {17,18,19,20} (doesn't include 21), {16,17,18,19} (no), so only {18,...,21} and {19,20,21,22} if we shift. Wait, {18,19,20,21} includes both 18 (div by 6) and 21 (div by 7). Also {17,18,19,20} doesn't include 21. {19,20,21,22} includes 21 but not 18. Therefore, only {18,19,20,21} is valid.

Wait, but difference between 18 and 21 is 3. Therefore, the four consecutive numbers must contain both numbers. So {18,19,20,21} is the only one. Similarly, for k=1: multiples 42*1 +18=60 and 42*1 +21=63. Four consecutive numbers containing both 60 and 63: {60,61,62,63}. Also {59,60,61,62} doesn't include 63. Therefore, only {60,61,62,63} is valid.

Therefore, Case 5 gives S: {18,19,20,21}, {60,61,62,63}.

Case 6: 6a -7b=3.

Solve 6a -7b=3.

6a =7b +3 => 6a ≡3 mod7 => 6a ≡3 mod7 => Multiply both sides by 6^{-1}=6: a≡18≡4 mod7. Therefore, a=7k +4.

Substitute back:

6*(7k +4)=7b +3 => 42k +24=7b +3 => 7b=42k +21 => b=6k +3.

Therefore, multiples:

6a=6*(7k +4)=42k +24

7b=7*(6k +3)=42k +21

Therefore, multiples are 42k +24 and 42k +21, difference of 3.

Check two-digit numbers:

Lower bound: 42k +21 ≥10 => k≥0.
